chunks.text: Pieter Abbeel is a Professor at UC Berkeley with research interests in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2008. His office is located at 746 Sutardja Dai Hall, and he can be contacted via email at pabbeel@cs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are arranged via email.
chunks.question: What are Pieter Abbeel's research interests?
chunks.answer: Artificial Intelligence and Control, Intelligent Systems, Robotics.

chunks.text: Ahmed Alaa is a Below The Line Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley. He earned his Ph.D. in EECS from UCLA in 2020. His contact email is amalaa@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Ahmed Alaa's academic position?

chunks.text: Krste AsanoviĂ„Â‡ is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley. His research interests include Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC), Integrated Circuits (INC), Operating Systems & Networking (OSNT), and Design, Modeling and Analysis (DMA). He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 1998. His office is at 579B Soda Hall, and he can be reached at krste@berkeley.edu. Office hours are by email appointment.
chunks.question: Where did Krste AsanoviĂ„Â‡ earn his Ph.D.?

chunks.text: Babak Ayazifar is a Teaching Professor at UC Berkeley with research interests in Education (EDUC) and Signal Processing (SP). He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from MIT in 2003. His office is at 517 Cory Hall, and his email is ayazifar@berkeley.edu. In Spring 2026, he teaches EECS 16A and EE 120.
chunks.question: What courses does Babak Ayazifar teach in Spring 2026?

chunks.text: Ruzena Bajcsy is a Professor Emerita at UC Berkeley with research interests in Artificial Intelligence (AI), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Graphics (GR), Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), and Security (SEC). She earned her Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 1972. Her office is at 719 Sutardja Dai Hall, and her email is bajcsy@seas.upenn.edu. Office hours are on Mondays and Wednesdays from 9-10 AM.
chunks.question: What is Ruzena Bajcsy's email address?

chunks.text: Michael Ball is a Continuing Lecturer at UC Berkeley with research interests in Education (EDUC) and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). He earned his M.S. in Computer Science from UC Berkeley in 2016. His office is at 784 Soda Hall, and his email is ball@berkeley.edu. Office hours are by appointment via email. In Spring 2026, he teaches CS 169L and CS 302.
chunks.question: What are Michael Ball's research interests?

chunks.text: David Bamman is a Below The Line Associate Professor at UC Berkeley. He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University in 2015. His contact email is dbamman@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is David Bamman's highest degree?

chunks.text: Brian A. Barsky is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley with research interests in Graphics (GR), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), and Signal Processing (SP). He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Utah in 1981. His office is at 443 Soda Hall, and his email is barsky@berkeley.edu. Office hours are by email appointment only.
chunks.question: Where did Brian Barsky earn his Ph.D.?

chunks.text: Peter Bartlett is a Professor Emeritus and Professor in the Graduate School at UC Berkeley with research interests in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Queensland, Australia in 1992. His office is at 723 Sutardja Dai Hall, and his email is peter@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Peter Bartlett's research focus?
chunks.answer: Artificial Intelligence and Control, Intelligent Systems, Robotics.

chunks.text: Alexandre Bayen is a Professor at UC Berkeley with research interests in Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Cyber-Physical Systems and Design Automation (CPSDA). He earned his Ph.D. in Aeronautics and Astronautics from Stanford University in 2003. His email is bayen@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Alexandre Bayen's email?
